title: Dreamy Gooey Brazilian Mussels Paella
description: A savory and nostalgic Brazilian dish featuring mussels, cooked to perfection in a tandoor, with a macrobiotic-friendly twist, combining the flavors of Brazil with the richness of paella.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in the tandoor over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the mussels, saffron threads, smoked paprika, and sea salt, and stir to combine.
Add the tomato paste and stir to combine.
Cook for 10 minutes, or until the mussels are cooked through and the sauce is gooey.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, paella is cooked over an open flame, but a tandoor can be used as a substitute.
Make sure to adjust the heat as needed to prevent burning.
